findings.title: XSS vulnerability: removed HTML sanitization on user-supplied comment content
findings.evidence: Line 2-3: Removed 'const safe = sanitizeHtml(raw);' and changed render from '{safe}' to '{raw}'. User-supplied comment text now renders without HTML escaping, enabling stored/reflected XSS attacks via malicious HTML in comment body.

notes: Diff Drift severity marked as LOW, but this is a critical security issue: direct rendering of unsanitized user input enables injection attacks.
Avatar.tsx change is formatting-only and carries no risk.
The change simplifies the code but at the cost of removing essential input validation.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 multi-model panel; blind, packet-only context.
